data.body: Consideration: [Reliability] next-batch must preserve (beyond the SKIPPED_* tokens listed in the epic SCs): (1) the OPUS_CAP=2 constant and SKIPPED_OPUS_CAP emission (controls usage-aware throttling — removing it blows the opus dispatch cap); (2) the OVERLAP_SAFE_FILES set (currently .test-index and similar shared-by-design files that MUST NOT trigger file-overlap serialization); (3) the human-readable conflict matrix printed to stderr when >=2 candidates contend for overlapping files (primary debugging tool for 'why was my batch serialized'). None of these are enumerated in the epic success criteria, but all three are production behavior of sprint-next-batch.sh and their omission would cause silent regressions. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
